text:
I'll search for information about AlpacaEval to verify the reference details.
Based on my web search results, I have found the referenced source. The GitHub repository at https://github.com/tatsu-lab/alpaca_eval contains a BibTeX citation block that explicitly lists the authors and title. The citation appears in multiple locations on the repository's documentation pages (README.md and other files).

The web search confirms that the cited reference exists and is a real software/project source. The GitHub repository shows the exact BibTeX entry:

"@misc{alpaca_eval, author = {Xuechen Li and Tianyi Zhang and Yann Dubois and Rohan Taori and Ishaan Gulrajani and Carlos Guestrin and Percy Liang and Tatsunori B. Hashimoto }, title = {AlpacaEval: An Automatic Evaluator of Instruction-following Models}, year = {2023}, month = {5}, publisher = {GitHub}, journal = {GitHub repository}, howpublished = {\url{https://github.com/tatsu-lab/alpaca_eval}}}"

The cited title "Alpacaeval: An automatic evaluator of instruction-following models" matches the actual title (allowing for minor case differences: "AlpacaEval" vs "Alpacaeval"), the authors match exactly, and the year (2023) matches. The automated checker's report that the author name "tatsu-lab" appears in the metadata reflects how GitHub attributes repository ownership, but the BibTeX entry on the repository itself clearly lists all the individual authors cited in the reference.

This is a non-academic source (software/dataset repository), which is a valid citation format. The repository exists at the URL provided, and the author list in the citation matches the authors listed in the official BibTeX citation block on the repository.

EXPLANATION: The reference is to a real software project on GitHub with matching title and authors in the official BibTeX citation embedded in the repository.
LINK: https://github.com/tatsu-lab/alpaca_eval
FOUND_TITLE: AlpacaEval: An Automatic Evaluator of Instruction-following Models
